title: Planning skill: interview-driven plan authoring for the P node
description:
Add a dedicated **planning skill** that owns the P (planning) node of the plan-before-fan-out bracket: turning a vague request into a plan-review-passing plan, ready for `jit-breakdown` to decompose.

## Context

JIT's skills cover breakdown (`jit-breakdown` = B node), execution (`jit-parallel`, `project-lead`), and lifecycle (`jit-manage`), but nothing owns **P** — authoring a plan good enough to pass `plan-review` and be decomposed. Today planning survives only as `jit-manage` Workflow B3 (a thin, optional design-doc template) and `project-lead`'s `references/architect-agent-prompt.md` (a one-shot doc generator dispatched inside the executor). P is the highest-leverage node — `plan-review` blocks breakdown, and a bad plan poisons every child — yet the least-supported phase.

This builds on the planning bracket delivered by epic `2fbd2a82` (P/B node types, `[planning]` config, the `plan-review` / `coverage-preview` / `breakdown-review` gates), which is why that epic is this task's dependency.

## Approach (settled design decisions)

- **Interview-first, cold-start capable.** The user is allowed to be vague. From a bare prompt with no goal/epic yet (e.g. "reproduce the results in this article"), the skill ingests any referenced artifact, then interviews to extract intent and converge on a testable definition of done before writing anything.
- **Orchestrator + dispatched roles.** `SKILL.md` is a thin orchestrator that runs the interview in the main loop and dispatches investigate / synthesize / review as sub-agents whose role prompts live in `references/` (the `project-lead` pattern), keeping the body within the agentskills size budget.
- **Rubric-aligned.** The synthesizer self-checks against the live `plan-review` rubric's four areas before leaving the gate PENDING for the standard runner.
- **One level at a time.** Planning recurses one hierarchy level per pass, interleaved with `jit-breakdown`; it emits a decomposition *sketch* but does not create child issues.
- **Purely additive.** No existing skill is modified; composition into `project-lead` Section 3A is deferred to a follow-up.

Design basis: six SDD / agentic-planning frameworks studied under `../jit-research` (BMAD-METHOD, cc-sdd, get-shit-done, OpenSpec, ruflo, superpowers).

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: From a vague cold-start prompt with no pre-existing goal/epic, the skill ingests any referenced artifact, interviews to extract intent, and converges on a confirmed definition of done. It does not fabricate a definition of done when intent stays underspecified — assumptions are surfaced or escalated.
- [hard] REQ-02: The skill creates the breakable container (type read from `[planning].breakable_types`, never hardcoded) with `[hard] REQ-NN` criteria in a `## Success Criteria` section, and brackets it with a planning node `P` at the configured `plan_doc_location`.
- [hard] REQ-03: The authored plan is structured to the four `plan-review` areas (criteria completeness; technical/architectural soundness grounded in actual code; coherent decomposition sketch with correct dependency ordering; risks + actionability); the skill self-reviews against that rubric and runs `plan-review`, iterating until its recorded status is `passed` before breakdown proceeds.
- [hard] REQ-04: `SKILL.md` is an orchestrator staying within the agentskills budget (<500 lines, ~5k tokens); investigate/synthesize/review run as dispatched sub-agents with prompts in `references/`.
- [hard] REQ-05: Planning recurses one level at a time and emits a decomposition sketch only — child-issue creation remains `jit-breakdown`'s job.
- [hard] REQ-06: No existing skill is modified by this task.

## Non-goals

- Performing the decomposition / creating child issues (remains `jit-breakdown`).
- Modifying `jit-manage`, `project-lead`, or other skills.
- Wiring the skill into `project-lead`'s autonomous flow (separate follow-up).
